Overview

Non-human Coverage

21%

Dimension pair combination coverage: 146 of 698 combinations have at least one character (substrate×size: 15/42, common_labels×form: 131/656).

Human Coverage

96%

HSN assumption coverage: 27 of 28 ability assumptions have at least one divergent human character.

Dimensions

Coverage of the 10 cognitive dimensions across non-human characters. Each chart shows how many characters use each category option within that dimension. The heatmap summarises which options are covered at all, and the gap tables highlight dimension-pair combinations absent from the current character set.

Distribution by Dimension

Processing Speed

Only one category defined: ms. All 24 characters use it.

Coverage Heatmap

Each cell shows whether that option is represented in at least one non-human character (blue = covered, white = not covered, grey = not applicable to this dimension).

Combination Gaps

Pairwise counts of non-human characters covering both category values. Highlighted rows (zero count) indicate combinations absent from the current set.

HSN Divergence

HSN (Human Standard Normal) divergence scores each human character against the ability assumptions embedded in the scenario design. Red cells indicate the character diverges from the normative assumption; blue cells indicate normative behaviour. Characters are ordered from least to most divergent.

HSN Divergence Heatmap

Rows = human characters (HID); columns = individual ability assumptions grouped by category. Red = divergent, blue = normative.

Divergent Assumptions per Character

Count of ability assumptions where each character diverges from the normative baseline, sorted from most to least divergent.
